title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.5)
statement_text: For every tree $T$ and complete graph $K$, there is an integer $c$ such that if $G$ is a graph containing neither $T$ nor $K$ as an induced subgraph, then $\chi(G) \leq c$.
context_text: This conjecture is invoked as the graph-theoretic motivation for the paper's study of induced restrictions in binary matroids, where matroids $I_t$ play the role of trees and projective geometries $PG(t-1,2)$ play the role of cliques. The paper notes the conjecture remains open in general, though proved for many specific trees $T$.
paper_contribution: Theorem 1.3 refutes the matroidal analogue of this conjecture by showing that for every integer $k$ there exists a matroid $M \in \mathcal{E}_3$ containing neither $I_3$ nor $F_7$ as an induced restriction with $\chi(M) \geq k$, establishing that bounding $\chi$ by excluding a fixed $I_s$ and a fixed $PG(t-1,2)$ as induced restrictions is impossible in general.
paper_title: The structure of binary matroids with no induced claw or Fano plane restriction

title: Conjecture 1.6
statement_text: For all $s \geq 1$ there exists an integer $k$ such that if $M$ is a simple binary matroid with no induced $I_s$-restriction or $PG(1, 2)$-restriction, then $\chi(M) \leq k$.
context_text: After showing that the full matroidal Gyárfás-Sumner analogue fails (Theorem 1.3), the authors conjecture that fixing $t = 2$—that is, excluding a triangle $PG(1,2)$ rather than an arbitrary projective geometry—restores a bound on the critical number. The conjecture is verified for $s \in \{1, 2, 3\}$ by Corollary 5.2, which gives $k = 1$; new ideas are needed for larger $s$.
paper_title: The structure of binary matroids with no induced claw or Fano plane restriction
